title: Daan-Ghati
tagline: Daan-Ghati — spiritual town of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Daan-Ghati is one of the two main temple structures in Govardhan, near Mathura, India.
overview.description: Daan-Ghati is one of the two main temple structures in Govardhan, near Mathura, India. The other temple structure is called Dasvisa. It is involved in the Govardhan Puja, a 21 kilometre circumambulation of the hill that it is believed Krishna lifted with his little finger to protect his worshipers from the wrath of Indra, the Vedic god of rain. Over time, this temple has slowly taken over the place of main temple of the town.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Magorra, Uttar Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Magorra is a town and a nagar panchayat in the Mathura district of the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Govardhan
topPlaces.description: Govardhan also called Giriraj, is a key pilgrimage centre in India and a municipal town; a nagar panchayat; seat of an MLA (Member of Legislative Assembly) of Uttar Pradesh; a Tehsil, in Mathura district in India in the state of Uttar Pradesh. About 23 kilometres from Mathura, the town is on the road link between Mathura and Deeg.

topPlaces.name: Adeeng, Uttar Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Adeeng, Ading is a town and a Gram panchayat in the Mathura district of the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Govardhan Hill
topPlaces.description: Govardhana Hill (Sanskrit: गोवर्धन; pronounced [ɡoːʋɐrdʰɐnɐ]), also called Mount Govardhana and Giriraj, is a sacred Hindu site in the Mathura district of Uttar Pradesh, India on an 8 km long hill located in the area of Govardhan and Radha Kund, which is about 21 kilometres (13 miles) from Vrindavan. It is the sacred centre of Braj and is identified as a natural form of Krishna, the Govardhana Shila.

topPlaces.name: Kusum Sarovar
topPlaces.description: Kusum Sarovar is considered in Hinduism a sacred water reservoir with a historic sand monument in its backdrop. It is situated on the holy Govardhan Hill between Manasi Ganga and Radha Kund in Mathura district of Uttar Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Radha Kund
topPlaces.description: Radha Kund (IAST: Rādhākuṇḍa, lit. 'Radha’s Pond') is a town and nagar panchayat in Mathura district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is also one of the pilgrimage sites dedicated to Hindu goddess Radha.

topPlaces.name: Borapa
topPlaces.description: Borapa also known as Borpa (Village ID 124249) is a small village located 20 km from Mathura in west and about 4 km from the town Sonkh in the state of Uttar Pradesh, India. According to the 2011 census it has a population of 2057 living in 330 households.

topPlaces.name: Bahaj
topPlaces.description: Bahaj is a village in Deeg tehsil of Bharatpur in Rajasthan, India. known for an ancient palaeochannel buried 23 metres under the village, which was discovered by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in 2025.

items.note: Magorra is a town and a nagar panchayat in the Mathura district of the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.

Govardhan also called Giriraj, is a key pilgrimage centre in India and a municipal town; a nagar panchayat; seat of an MLA (Member of Legislative Assembly) of Uttar Pradesh; a Tehsil, in Mathura district in India in the state of Uttar Pradesh. About 23 kilometres from Mathura, the town is on the road link between Mathura and Deeg.
Adeeng, Ading is a town and a Gram panchayat in the Mathura district of the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.

Govardhana Hill (Sanskrit: गोवर्धन; pronounced [ɡoːʋɐrdʰɐnɐ]), also called Mount Govardhana and Giriraj, is a sacred Hindu site in the Mathura district of Uttar Pradesh, India on an 8 km long hill located in the area of Govardhan and Radha Kund, which is about 21 kilometres (13 miles) from Vrindavan. It is the sacred centre of Braj and is identified as a natural form of Krishna, the Govardhana Shila.

Kusum Sarovar is considered in Hinduism a sacred water reservoir with a historic sand monument in its backdrop. It is situated on the holy Govardhan Hill between Manasi Ganga and Radha Kund in Mathura district of Uttar Pradesh, India.

Radha Kund (IAST: Rādhākuṇḍa, lit. 'Radha’s Pond') is a town and nagar panchayat in Mathura district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is also one of the pilgrimage sites dedicated to Hindu goddess Radha.

Borapa also known as Borpa (Village ID 124249) is a small village located 20 km from Mathura in west and about 4 km from the town Sonkh in the state of Uttar Pradesh, India. According to the 2011 census it has a population of 2057 living in 330 households.

Bahaj is a village in Deeg tehsil of Bharatpur in Rajasthan, India. known for an ancient palaeochannel buried 23 metres under the village, which was discovered by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in 2025.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Daan-Ghati?
faq.a: The best time to visit Daan-Ghati is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

The nearest airport is Agra Airport (Pandit Deen Dayal Upadhyay) (62 km) and the nearest railway station is Agra Cantt (64 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Daan-Ghati?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Daan-Ghati at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Daan-Ghati?
faq.a: Stays in Daan-Ghati start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Daan-Ghati?
faq.a: The top attractions are Magorra, Uttar Pradesh, Govardhan, Adeeng, Uttar Pradesh, Govardhan Hill, Kusum Sarovar.
